2604.19468.title: Fairness Audits of Institutional Risk Models in Deployed ML Pipelines
2604.19468.paper_summary: Fairness audits of institutional risk models are critical for understanding how deployed machine learning pipelines allocate resources. Drawing on multi-year collaboration with Centennial College, where our prior ethnographic work introduced the ASP-HEI Cycle, we present a replica-based audit of a deployed Early Warning System (EWS), replicating its model using institutional training data and design specifications. We evaluate disparities by gender, age, and residency status across the full pipeline (training data, model predictions, and post-processing) using standard fairness metrics. Our audit reveals systematic misallocation: younger, male, and international students are disproportionately flagged for support, even when many ultimately succeed, while older and female students with comparable dropout risk are under-identified. Post-processing amplifies these disparities by collapsing heterogeneous probabilities into percentile-based risk tiers. This work provides a replicable methodology for auditing institutional ML systems and shows how disparities emerge and compound across stages, highlighting the importance of evaluating construct validity alongside statistical fairness. It contributes one empirical thread to a broader program investigating algorithms, student data, and power in higher education.
2604.19468.paper_summary_zh: 公平性審核機構風險模型對於理解已部署的機器學習管道如何分配資源至關重要。基於與百年學院的多年合作，我們的先前人種誌工作介紹了ASP-HEI循環，我們展示了已部署的早期預警系統（EWS）的基於複製的審核，使用機構訓練數據和設計規範來複製其模型。我們使用標準公平性指標評估整個管道中的性別、年齡和居住狀況的差異（訓練數據、模型預測和後處理）。我們的審核揭示了系統性錯誤分配：年輕的男性和國際學生在需要支持時被不成比例地標記，即使許多人最終成功，而年齡較大和女性學生在可比的退學風險下卻被低估識別。後處理通過將異質概率壓縮為基於百分位的風險級別來放大這些差異。這項工作提供了一種可複製的方法論，用於審核機構機器學習系統，並顯示差異如何在各階段中出現和累積，突顯了在統計公平性之外評估構建有效性的重要性。它為更廣泛的計劃提供了一個實證線索，該計劃調查高等教育中的算法、學生數據和權力。
2604.19468.author: Kelly McConvey et.al.
2604.19468.authors: Kelly McConvey, Dipto Das, Maya Ghai, Angelina Zhai, Rosa Lee, Shion Guha
